name: zinput_mouse_init_device
description: zInput::Mouse_InitDevice creates the mouse device, upgrades it to IDirectInputDevice2A, configures format/cooperative level/buffering, activates acquisition, and centers cursor state.
known_limits: Reviewed VC byte comparison from python tools/recoil_vc6_verify.py zinput_mouse_init_device failed under VC5SP3 11.00.7022 /O2 /Ob0 /Zp4: 127 unmasked mismatches, 80 relocation-masked bytes, BN size 226, VC object size 240, 10 trailing VC NOPs trimmed; see build/vc6-verify/zinput_mouse_init_device/verify/4701f0_byte_triage.txt.

The reviewed byte drift is stack-slot and scheduling shape around the local base device pointer, DirectInput property struct setup, and helper-call tail; the native smoke verifies create/query/release, format/cooperative-level/property setup, acquisition, client rect update, and recentering.
